exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1. Lie on an incline bench set at 45 degrees holding the dumbbells up over your chest at 90 degrees to the ground with your arms straight.

2. Lower the dumbbells to the sides of your body in an arc-like motion inline with the middle of your chest, until your hands are at the same level as your chest.

3. Raise the dumbbells back up over your chest, keeping your arms straight throughout.

4. Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench High Row
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on an incline bench with the dumbbells hanging at your sides, your arms straight and your palms facing down.

2.Curl the weight up to your chest, bending your elbows straight back close to your sides.

3.Lower down returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.description:
1) Sit on an incline bench set at 45 degrees with dumbbells resting on both thighs. 

2) Start with both dumbbells at shoulder level. 

3) Press the weights upwards over your upper chest until the weights meet at the top. 

4) Lower the weights slowly and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Row
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on an incline bench with the dumbbells hanging at your sides, your arms straight and your palms facing in.

2.Curl the weights up to shoulder level.

3.Lower down returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) Sit on an incline bench with arms hanging and dumbbells in both hands, palms facing in. 

2) Curl the weight in your right hand up to shoulder level, while turning your wrist so that your palms face upwards at the top. 

3) Slowly lower the weight and alternate.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Tricep Press
exercises.description:
Lie on an incline bench with dumbbells close together. Perform a narrow chest press, emphasizing the triceps and upper chest. 

The incline adds an additional challenge to the upper body.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Internal Rotation
exercises.description:
1.Lie on your side holding a dumbbell in your lower hand, palm facing up with the elbow bent at 90 degrees away from your body.

2.Raise the dumbbell up to the opposite shoulder, keeping your elbow at 90 degrees and your upper arm at your side.

3.Slowly lower the dumbbell returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Iron Cross Raise
exercises.alternateName: Shoulder Front side front opener raise
exercises.description:
Hold dumbbells at your sides. Simultaneously lift arms laterally and raise them outward to form a cross-like position. Hold briefly, then lower. 

Engage your core and shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Isometric Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) Grab a dumbbell in both hands and curl the left arm so it sits at a 90 degree angle to your body. 

2) Hold that position in your left arm and start by curling the weight in your right hand toward your shoulder, pause, and then lower to start position. 

3) Complete the full set curling only your right hand while leaving the left arm still at 90 degrees. 

4) Switch arms and repeat

exercises.name: Dumbbell Jump Squat to Press
exercises.alternateName: Dumbbell Jump Squat to Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Holding a dumbbell in each hand at your shoulders, lower into a squat, then jump up explosively. After landing, stand up fully, then press the dumbbells overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Kickstand Deadlift
exercises.description: Start in a kickstand position. Using dumbbells, hinge to lower the weights and stand tall.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Kickstand Deadlift to Single Arm Row
exercises.description: Start in a kickstand position and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite to the forward leg. Hinge to lower the weight then row the weight toward your ribs. Stand tall after performing the row.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge to Arnold Press
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing inward. Step one foot out to the side into a lateral lunge, bending the lead knee while keeping the other leg straight. 

Push through the lead heel to return to the standing position. As you press the dumbbells overhead, rotate your palms outward. 

Rotate your palms back inward as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder level.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge to Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step out into a lateral lunge, then curl the weights to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge to Press
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level. Step one leg out to the side into a lateral lunge, keeping the opposite leg straight and lowering your hips back and down. 

Push through the bent leg to return to the standing position, then immediately press the dumbbells overhead. Lower the weights back to shoulder level and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge to Press
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing inward. 

Step one foot out to the side into a lateral lunge, bending the lead knee while keeping the other leg straight.

Push explosively through the lead heel to return to standing, driving the trailing leg forward into a knee drive while simultaneously pressing the dumbbells overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge to Press
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing inward. 

Step one foot out to the side into a lateral lunge, bending the lead knee while keeping the other leg straight. 

Push through the lead heel to return to the standing position, then press the dumbbells overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge to Single Arm Lateral Raise
exercises.description: Holding a dumbbell in one hand, step out to the side into a lateral lunge. After you push back to stand, lift the dumbbell out to the side to shoulder height

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge with Curl
exercises.description: Holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, step out to the side into a lateral lunge. As you bend your knees, curl the dumbbells up to your shoulders. Push back to standing and lower the weights, then step out with the other leg and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
1) Stand and bend forward slightly with your knees bent too. 

2) With dumbbells in both hands and elbows bent, raise upper arms to sides until elbows are at shoulder height. 

3) Pause, lower and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Step Up
exercises.description:
Stand beside a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand. 

Step sideways onto the bench with one foot, driving through your heel to lift your body up. 

Step back down with control and alternate sides.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Step-Up to Curl
exercises.alternateName: Dumbbell Lateral Step Up to Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, step up and stand tall on the platform, then curl the weights to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Laying Tricep Extension to Press
exercises.description:
Lie on the floor holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Begin with your elbows bent and dumbbells above your head. 

Perform a tricep extension by lowering the dumbbells behind your head, then push them back up until your arms are fully extended. Lower the dumbbells down to chest level so your elbows rest on the floor. 

From this position, press the dumbbells straight up into a chest press until your arms are fully extended. Lower the dumbbells back to chest level with elbows on the floor and repeat for reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Leg extension
exercises.description: Dumbbell Leg extension

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lunge to Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing inward. 

Step forward into a forward lunge, bending both knees until the front thigh is parallel to the floor and the rear knee hovers above the ground. 

Push through the front heel to return to standing, and as you rise, lift the dumbbells out to the sides to shoulder height with arms fully extended.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Down Reverse Fly
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on a bench holding a dumbbell on each hand, arms hanging at your sides, palms facing in.

2.Raise your arms to the side until your elbow is at shoulder height and your arm is roughly parallel to the floor.

3.Lower down returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Down Row
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on a bench, holding a dumbbell on each hand, arms straight, hanging at your sides.

2.Lift the dumbbells up to the sides of your chest, bending at the elbows.Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Down Tricep Kickback
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on a bench holding a dumbbell on each hand with your elbows bent at 90 degrees up at shoulder height.

2.Raise the dumbbells up and back, straightening your arms.

3.Lower the dumbbells, keeping your shoulders steady, returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Rear Delt Raise
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, you’ll be primarily working the deltoids and mid back muscles.

2.When you do the movement, you wanna articulate from the shoulder as best as you can

3.While holding the dumbbells, lay with your chest down on a slightly inclined (around 15 degrees when measured from the floor) adjustable bench. 

4.Position the palms of the hands in a neutral manner (palms facing your torso) as you keep the arms extended with the elbow slightly bent. This will be your starting position.

5.Now, raise your arms to the side until your elbows are at shoulder height and your arms are roughly parallel to the floor. 

6.Slowly lower the dumbbell to the starting position.

7.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Rear Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
1) Start by lying your chest down on a flat bench and grasp dumbbells with each hand. 

2) Raise your upper arms to your sides until your hands reach shoulder height, pause, lower and repeat. 

3. Keep your elbows set at a single angle with soft bend.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1) Lay back on a flat bench and hold a dumbbell with both hands palms facing up. 

2) Start by holding the dumbbell with your arms straight upwards. 

3) While keeping your elbows still, lower the dumbbell until it is behind your head. 

4) Once the dumbbell goes just past your head, use your triceps to lift the dumbbell back to the starting position. 

5) Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Overhead Standing March
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand overhead with arms fully extended. 

Keep your core engaged and shoulders stable. Lift one knee toward your chest in a controlled march, then lower it and lift the other knee. 

Continue alternating knees while maintaining the dumbbells overhead throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Palm Down Wrist Curl
exercises.description:
1.Start by kneeling beside a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Place forearms flat on the bench palms down, with your wrists on the edge of the bench.

2.Begin by lowering dumbbells as far as possible, pause.

3.Curl dumbbells up as high as possible. Keep your forearms still during the whole exercise.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Palm Up Wrist Curl
exercises.description:
1.Start by kneeling beside a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Place forearms flat on the bench palms up, with your wrists on the edge of the bench.

2.Begin by lowering dumbbells as far as possible, pause.

3.Curl dumbbells up as high as possible. Keep your forearms still during the whole exercise.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Pullover
exercises.description:
1) Lie back on a flat bench and hold dumbbells over head with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent. 

2) Without moving your elbows, lower the dumbbells behind head, pause, and return to starting position by pulling the dumbbells back up over head.

exercises.name: DUMBBELL PULLOVER FOR THE LATS
exercises.description: DUMBBELL PULLOVER FOR THE LATS

exercises.name: Dumbbell Push Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, dip at the knees and drive the weights overhead (push press).

exercises.name: Dumbbell RDL To Rear Delt Fly
exercises.alternateName: Dumbbell deadlift To Rear Delt Fly
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Hinge at your hips and lower the dumbbells toward the floor while keeping a slight bend in your knees and a straight back (Romanian Deadlift). 

When you reach the bottom of the hinge, engage your glutes and hamstrings to slightly rise and, while maintaining the hip hinge, lift the dumbbells out to your sides with elbows slightly bent (rear delt fly). 

Lower the dumbbells back down while still in the hinge and return to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell RDL to Upright Row
exercises.description:
Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs with palms facing your body. With a slight bend in your knees, hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells toward mid-shin level, keeping your back straight and core braced. 

Engage your hamstrings and glutes to return to a standing position, then immediately perform an upright row by pulling the dumbbells upward along your torso until your elbows reach shoulder height. 

Lower the weights back down under control and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Renegade Row
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, maintain tight abdominals and tight glutes. 

2) Keep your hands center of your chest throughout the exercise. 

3) Wide feet give you a better stable base. 

4) Position yourself on your toes and your hands as though you were doing a pushup, with the body straight and extended.  

5) Push one dumbbell into the floor and row the other dumbbell, retracting the shoulder blade of the working side as you flex the elbow, pulling it to your side. 

6) Lower the dumbbell to the floor and begin the dumbbell in the opposite hand.  

7) Repeat for several reps.
